properties.Name: Admiralty LegCo
properties.Description: Protesters occupied government district — transforming civic space into resistance territory

properties.Name: Mong Kok Streets
properties.Description: Protesters reclaimed commercial streets from state control — redefining public space

properties.Name: PolyU Siege
properties.Description: Protesters fortified university campus — repurposing educational space as refuge and frontline

properties.Name: CUHK Campus
properties.Description: Students defended campus as space of both learning and resistance — identity fused with place

properties.Name: Airport Occupation
properties.Description: Protesters shut down international gateway — disrupting Hong Kong global image and economy

properties.Name: Admiralty MTR Hub
properties.Description: Dense transit network channeled thousands of protesters rapidly across city — infrastructure enabled resistance

properties.Name: Causeway Bay Density
properties.Description: Commercial density enabled rapid dispersal and crowd camouflage tactics — urban form as protest tool

properties.Name: Victoria Park
properties.Description: Traditional civic space whose NSL closure signaled shrinking of public life — absence shaped identity

properties.Name: Civic Square Fenced
properties.Description: Post-2014 fencing physically prevented gathering — denial of space shaped protest tactics

properties.Name: Lion Rock
properties.Description: Hillside landscape became political symbol — topography amplified protest visibility globally

properties.Name: NSL Zone Central
properties.Description: After June 2020 — criminalization of protest reshaped everyday movement and identity in core districts

properties.Name: NSL Zone Mong Kok
properties.Description: High surveillance NSL enforcement changed how residents use and experience public space
